Build + install signed Debug-Recorder build

remaining_tasks.progress: Scriptable tier FULL PASS (Music pauses AND resumes in plain+aiCleanup — user confirmed both). Mute tier still to test: user chose option A (switch default output to MacBook Pro Speakers, replay YouTube mute test). User confirmed the DAC has no software mute/volume.

remaining_tasks.name: Record 30-03-UAT-RESULTS.md verdict (with documented DAC limitation); on PASS update ROADMAP + CHANGELOG + AppBuildInfo.swift + write SUMMARY

blockers.description: User's default output device is a JDS Labs external DAC (hardware-volume only). CoreAudio mute, CoreAudio volumeScalar, AND AppleScript 'set volume output muted' are ALL unsupported on it (probe: mute/volumeScalar HasProperty == false on every scope; 'get volume settings' returns output muted/volume = missing value). The mute-output fallback (MEDIA-PAUSE-03) is therefore physically impossible to validate on this device — macOS itself cannot mute it. The code is correct and would work on a software-controllable output (built-in speakers, AirPods, HDMI).
blockers.workaround: Switch default output to MacBook Pro Speakers and re-run the YouTube mute test to validate the code, then document hardware-volume DACs as an unsupported edge case.

human_actions_pending.action: On resume: set System Settings > Sound > Output to MacBook Pro Speakers, play a YouTube video in Safari, run the mute test (HOLD mutes / RELEASE unmutes) + the user-muted-system guard.
human_actions_pending.context: User chose option A and will do this on resume. This is the ONLY remaining UAT check — scriptable tier and Music resume are already confirmed PASS.

decisions.decision: Execute Phase 30 sequentially on the main working tree, no worktree executors.
decisions.rationale: Project memory reference_dicticus_build_and_gsd_execution: worktree agents branch from a stale base and .planning/ is gitignored so disk-only SUMMARYs get orphaned. Set workflow.use_worktrees=false for the run.

decisions.decision: Added an AppleScript mute fallback (set volume output muted / output muted of get volume settings) when CoreAudio kAudioDevicePropertyMute is unavailable.
decisions.rationale: Original 30-02 implemented CoreAudio-only; the plan required the AppleScript fallback for devices without a master-mute property. Real bug, fixed in 9a4da59.

decisions.decision: Mute fallback cannot be made to work on the user's JDS Labs DAC.
decisions.rationale: Empirically probed: no software mute/volume control exists on the device at any scope. This is a macOS/hardware limitation, not a code defect.

next_action: User chose option A and confirmed Music pause+resume. On resume: have the user switch default output to MacBook Pro Speakers and run the YouTube mute test; on PASS write 30-03-UAT-RESULTS.md (verdict PASS + documented 'hardware-volume DACs unsupported' limitation), then run Task 3 (ROADMAP/CHANGELOG/AppBuildInfo + SUMMARY), then phase verification.
context_notes: Phase 30 is one plan from done. Waves 1+2 built and committed cleanly; signed build installed. The ONLY open item is the mute-tier UAT, blocked purely by the user's external DAC having no software volume/mute. Scriptable tier (Music pause) already works. Do NOT re-debug the mute code — it was investigated to ground truth (CoreAudio + AppleScript both unsupported on deviceID 120). The build/install workflow: cd macOS && xcodegen generate && xcodebuild Debug-Recorder ... then ./scripts/install-local.sh (re-signs Developer ID). Test signing flags: CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO. Do NOT commit project.pbxproj churn.
